Notice Of Motion And Motion For Leave To File Cross Complaint
Off Calendar Dec-27-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT UNICORN CLUB, LTD.'s Motion For Leave To File Cross Complaint.

Defendant Unicorn Club, LTD.'s motion for leave to file cross-complaint is GRANTED. The interest of justice is served and leave is properly granted under CCP section 428.50(c) where a proposed cross-complaint is based on facts put in issue by the complaint and answer. (Danzinger v. Peebler (1948) 88 Cal.App.2d 307, 310.) Plaintiff Ross Moore has not demonstrated any prejudice in granting leave to Defendant to file the cross-complaint. This motion was made only eight months after the complaint was filed, six months after answer filed, and only weeks after obtaining the documents and testimony needed by Defendant to support its claims. Moreover, the trial date is still several months away. Glogau v. Hagan (1951) 107 Cal.App.2d 313 does not stand for the proposition that the court should not grant leave to file any cross-complaint that might be subject to a demurrer. Rather, the case looked to various factors, including an 11 month delay from time of answer to complaint, the existence of a prior action based upon causes alleged in the cross-complaint that was still pending, as well as the fact that the claims were demurrable. The merits of the claims in the cross-complaint cannot be litigated until the cross-complaint is filed and the parties have satisfied their pre-demurrer meet and confer obligations under CCP section 430.41.

An order is signed for consolidated cases. (see scanned order for details)

Judge: Newton Lam, Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Anthony Vaughn, CSR#6185, tonyvaughn@yahoo.com, Reported. =(302/NL)
